subject: Chemistry
q: A certain orbital has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo></math> The number of radial nodes in this orbital is _______. (Round off to the Nearest Integer).
question: A certain orbital has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo></math> The number of radial nodes in this orbital is _______. (Round off to the Nearest Integer).
solution:
n=4 and mℓ=-3

Hence, ℓ value must be 3.

Now, number of radial nodes =n-ℓ-1

=4-3-1=0

subject: Chemistry
q: Outermost electronic configuration of a group <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>13</mn></math> element, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi><mo>,</mo></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mn>1</mn></msup><mo>.</mo></math> The electronic configuration of an element of p-block period-five placed diagonally to element, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi></math> is:
question: Outermost electronic configuration of a group <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>13</mn></math> element, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi><mo>,</mo></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mn>1</mn></msup><mo>.</mo></math> The electronic configuration of an element of p-block period-five placed diagonally to element, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi></math> is:
solution: The element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Ga</mi></math> and the diagonal element of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>5</mn><mtext>th </mtext></msup></math> period is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mmultiscripts><mi>Sn</mi><mprescripts/><mn>50</mn><none/></mmultiscripts></math> having outer electronic configuration will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>[</mo><mi>Kr</mi><mo>]</mo><mn>5</mn><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></msup><mn>4</mn><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi></mrow><mn>10</mn></msup><mn>5</mn><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>.</mo></math>

subject: Chemistry
q: Consider an imaginary ion <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mmultiscripts><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><none/><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow><mprescripts/><mn>22</mn><mn>48</mn></mmultiscripts></math>. The nucleus contains '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math>'<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>%</mo></math> more neutrons than the number of electrons in the ion. The value of '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math>' is
question: Consider an imaginary ion <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mmultiscripts><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><none/><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow><mprescripts/><mn>22</mn><mn>48</mn></mmultiscripts></math>. The nucleus contains '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math>'<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>%</mo></math> more neutrons than the number of electrons in the ion. The value of '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></math>' is
solution:
X22483-

In an atomic representation XZA

Z is atomic number = 22

A is atomic mass number = 48.

No. of protons  = Z = 22

No. of neutrons =A-Z = 48-22 = 26

No. of electrons =P-charge = 22+3 = 25

% of extra neutrons than electrons =26-2525×100=4 % = a%

So, the value of a=4.

subject: Chemistry
q: For a metal ion, the calculated magnetic moment is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>90</mn><mo> </mo><mi>BM</mi></math>. This metal ion has _______ number of unpaired electrons
question: For a metal ion, the calculated magnetic moment is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>90</mn><mo> </mo><mi>BM</mi></math>. This metal ion has _______ number of unpaired electrons
solution:
The magnetic moment of the metal ion can be calculated using

μ=nn+2BM

n=number of unpaired electrons.

μ=4.90 BM

4.90BM=(n)(n+2) BM

24 = (n) (n + 2)

n = 4

subject: Chemistry
q: Assume that the radius of the first Bohr orbit of hydrogen atom is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>Å</mo></math>. The radius of the third Bohr orbit of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>He</mi><mo>+</mo></msup></math> is _____ picometer. (Nearest Integer)
question: Assume that the radius of the first Bohr orbit of hydrogen atom is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>Å</mo></math>. The radius of the third Bohr orbit of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>He</mi><mo>+</mo></msup></math> is _____ picometer. (Nearest Integer)
solution:
For hydrogen like species, from Bohr's formulas we get,
r∝n2Z
where n is orbit number, Z is atomic number

Thus,

⇒rHe*=rH×n2Z

⇒rHe+=0.6×322

=2.7Å=270 × 10-12 m

∴rHe+=270pm

subject: Chemistry
q: Aqueous solution of which of the following salts will not contain ions with the electronic configuration <math><mn>1</mn><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mn>2</mn><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mn>2</mn><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msup><mn>3</mn><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mn>3</mn><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msup></math> ?
question: Aqueous solution of which of the following salts will not contain ions with the electronic configuration <math><mn>1</mn><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mn>2</mn><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mn>2</mn><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msup><mn>3</mn><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mn>3</mn><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msup></math> ?
solution:
NaF →Na+→1s2 2s2 2p6F-→1s2 2s2 2p6 Not Match

KBr →K+=1s22s22p63s23p6Br-=1s22s22p63s23p64s24p6

NaCl →Na+=1s22s22p6Cl=1s22s22p63s23p6

CaI2→Ca+2=1s22s22p63s23p6I-=Kr4d105s25p6

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are the quantum numbers for 4 electrons.

A. n=3,l=2,m1=1,ms=+12

B. n=4,l=1,m1=0,ms=+12

C. n=4,l=2,m1=-2,ms=-12

D. n=3,l=1,m1=-1,ms=+12

The correct order of increasing energy is
question:
Given below are the quantum numbers for 4 electrons.

A. n=3,l=2,m1=1,ms=+12

B. n=4,l=1,m1=0,ms=+12

C. n=4,l=2,m1=-2,ms=-12

D. n=3,l=1,m1=-1,ms=+12

The correct order of increasing energy is
solution:
Energy order of sub-shell decided by n+l value.

Greater the n+l value of a sub-shell greater is its energy.

If two sub-shells have same n+l values the orbital with greater principal quantum number has greater energy

A⇒3 d⇒n+l=3+2=5

B⇒4p⇒n+l=4+1=5

C⇒4 d⇒n+l=4+2=6

D⇒3s⇒n+l=3+0=3

D<A<B<C
